blurb: Apple platforms support enterprise Single Sign-on through three complementary mechanisms. The Kerberos SSO extension integrates natively with Active Directory, obtaining TGTs using device certificates or user credentials and transparently presenting service tickets to Kerberos-protected resources. The Extensible SSO framework allows identity providers to deploy MDM-configured SSO extensions supporting SAML 2.0 and OpenID Connect for seamless authentication to web and native apps. Platform SSO on macOS binds local account authentication to enterprise identity provider credentials, with tokens issued by the IdP enabling unified corporate identity across all Mac sign-in contexts.
